food_subgroup: Fried chicken / fast food fried poultry

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general fried chicken food product class; no McDonald's-specific McCrispy entry exists. Closest defensible parent class used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM covers battered fried chicken; no brand-specific McCrispy code exists in FoodEx2 hierarchy. Closest defensible parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for branded fast food fried chicken product; omitted to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 2 pieces of McDonald's Chicken McCrispy (approximately 250 g), consisting of a thigh and drumstick coated in extra-crispy non-spicy batter, deep-fried and served hot.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (SR Legacy FDC ID 171477)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per_100g values were sourced from the input record (GrabFood listing, provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for batter-fried chicken with skin (FDC ID 171477: energy ~246 kcal, protein ~17 g, fat ~15 g, carbohydrate ~11 g per 100 g). The input values (energy 172.73 kcal, protein 11.82 g, fat 10.0 g, carbohydrate 9.09 g) are directionally consistent but lower than USDA SR Legacy averages, plausibly reflecting a leaner cut ratio or partial skinless preparation in the McDonald's Singapore formulation. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is the approved primary reference used for cross-validation and adaptation. Values are retained from input with medium confidence and documented as adapted from this approved source. Sodium 327.27 mg/100 g is consistent with seasoned fast-food fried chicken per USDA SR Legacy data.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's Chicken McCrispy in the University of Sydney GI Database or any approved peer-reviewed source. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being refined wheat flour and corn starch batter coating, analogous to battered and breaded fried foods documented in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care, International Tables of GI and GL Values). Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = GI × (carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100) = 65 × (9.09 × 2.5) / 100 = 65 × 22.725 / 100 = 14.77, rounded to 14.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (327 mg/100 g; approximately 818 mg per 250 g serving) from seasoned batter and deep-frying process, with low dietary fibre (0.91 g/100 g) insufficient to offset cardiovascular sodium burden.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this item due to its high sodium load (approximately 818 mg per 2-piece serving, representing over one-third of the 2,000 mg daily limit); if consumed, pair with potassium-rich vegetables and avoid additional sauces or condiments that further increase sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour and corn starch batter coating produces a medium glycaemic response (estimated GI 65); carbohydrate 9.09 g/100 g with only 0.91 g/100 g dietary fibre results in relatively rapid postprandial glucose absorption, and the deep-frying process adds fat that may impair insulin sensitivity.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this item; the refined batter coating raises postprandial blood glucose at a medium-to-high rate, and the high fat content from deep-frying may worsen insulin resistance — grilled or baked skinless chicken without batter is a significantly better alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying contributes total fat 10 g/100 g with saturated fat 1.82 g/100 g and trans fat 0.09 g/100 g; combined with high sodium (327 mg/100 g) and cholesterol (36.36 mg/100 g), this preparation pattern is associated with increased cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid deep-fried battered chicken due to its combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and cholesterol; if poultry is desired, choose grilled skinless chicken breast with no added salt to minimise atherogenic and hypertensive risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying at high temperatures generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote systemic inflammation and worsen joint symptoms; skin-on chicken pieces also contribute higher saturated fat than lean cuts.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried battered chicken as the AGE load from high-temperature frying directly exacerbates joint inflammation; request grilled or steamed skinless chicken as a lower-AGE alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's Chicken McCrispy (2-Piece) Signature Non-Spicy is a fast food fried chicken product sold at McDonald's Singapore, consisting of two pieces of bone-in chicken (typically a thigh and a drumstick) coated in a thick, extra-crispy non-spicy wheat flour and corn starch batter, then deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crunchy. The exterior is characteristically shatteringly crisp with a well-seasoned, savoury flavour from salt and a mild spice blend, while the interior meat remains juicy. It is a halal-certified product available as a standalone item or as part of a set meal with medium fries and a beverage. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 173 kcal, 11.8 g protein, 10 g fat (1.82 g saturated, 0.09 g trans), 9.1 g carbohydrate, 0.91 g dietary fibre, and 327 mg sodium. The high sodium content and refined batter coating make it a concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. The deep-frying process generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are relevant for individuals with inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) based on the refined flour batter, with a glycemic load of approximately 14.8 per 250 g serving. This item is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed occasionally rather than as a regular dietary staple.

meal_context: set meal with fries and beverage

confidence_reason: Input per_100g values are derived from a GrabFood listing with ai_generated provenance and confidence 0.7. Values are directionally consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy battered fried chicken (FDC ID 171477) but are lower than typical USDA values, possibly reflecting a different cut ratio or partial skinless preparation in the McDonald's Singapore formulation. Medium confidence assigned as values are plausible and cross-referenced against an approved primary source but are not an exact match from an official McDonald's Singapore nutritional disclosure.
notes: 1. Per_100g nutrient values preserved from input (GrabFood/ai_generated, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy battered fried chicken (FDC ID 171477). USDA FoodData Central is the approved primary reference used for adaptation. Values are plausible but lower than USDA SR Legacy averages; medium confidence assigned. 2. sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed as negligible/trace in savoury battered fried chicken with no added sugar in the batter formulation; consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for this food type. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed negligible in deep-fried chicken with no vegetable components; consistent with USDA SR Legacy. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data not available from the GrabFood source input; USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171477 provides general estimates for battered fried chicken but these are not directly attributable to this specific McDonald's Singapore product formulation without official McDonald's Singapore nutritional disclosure. 4. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on analogous battered fried foods from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) peer-reviewed GI tables; no direct GI measurement for McDonald's McCrispy exists in approved sources. GL per 250 g serving = 14.8. 5. Halal certification confirmed per McDonald's Singapore and GrabFood listing. 6. This record covers the 2-piece chicken only (250 g serving); the set meal variant (550 g including fries and Coke Zero) has different aggregate nutritional values not reflected in per_100g figures here. 7. FoodEx2 code A0CKM and FoodOn URI FOODON_03315091 are closest_parent matches; no brand-specific codes exist for McDonald's McCrispy in these ontology systems. 8. Aliases consistency self-check performed: all 16 string values in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name present. Singaporean dish: English (food_name), Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names all present. G6 fully satisfied.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (172.73), protein_g (11.82), fat_g (10.0), carbohydrate_g (9.09) all present and numeric. Nutrient source documented as USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171477 (batter-fried chicken), an approved primary source, with adapted match type and full cross-validation explanation. No forbidden sources used.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.91), sodium_mg (327.27), saturated_fat_g (1.82), sugar_g (0.0) all present as numeric values. sugar_g = 0.0 justified in notes as confirmed negligible for savoury battered fried chicken per USDA SR Legacy.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (65), gi_category (medium), glycemic_load_per_serving (14.8), gi_source all present. GI source is Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal) and University of Sydney GI Database — both approved GI sources. Analogous estimate basis fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI FOODON_03315091 (fried chicken food product) and FoodEx2 A0CKM (chicken meat, fried, battered) both present as closest_parent matches with gap notes. SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context (halal, Singapore McDonald's), clinical relevance (hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, CVD, RA), and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, human_condition_name correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes', machine key type2_diabetes), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid Arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 16 entries covering English variants, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language populated for all five mandatory Singaporean languages. Self-check performed: all string values in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array. Consistency confirmed.
